name: First-Time User Access to Critical Asset
notes: Before enabling this rule, add systems to be monitored to the Critical Assets reference set. To make use of it, enable it for a 'learning' period of time such that the User-System Authentication or Usage reference collection will establish normal access patterns. After this period, edit the rule response to "Ensure the dispatched event is part of an offense" and make sure that the offense will be indexed on Username.
conditions_text: when an event matches any of the following BB:CategoryDefinition: Authentication Success
when any of Destination IP are contained in any of Critical Assets - IP
when any of Username is the key and any of Destination IP is the value in any of User-System Authentication or Usage - IP
when the event username matches the following [^\w\d]*
TD_list: APPLY First-Time User Access to Critical Asset on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when an event matches any of the following BB:CategoryDefinition: Authentication Success
AND when any of Destination IP are contained in any of Critical Assets - IP
AND NOT when any of Username is the key and any of Destination IP is the value in any of User-System Authentication or Usage - IP
AND when the event username matches the following [^\w\d]*
RED_list: Event name: First-Time User Access to Critical Asset
Event description: This event indicates that the given user is accessing this system for the first time. This may be benign but could indicate a user attempting unauthorized access.
Add "destinationIP" to Reference Map Of Sets: "User-System Authentication or Usage" with key "userName"
